assertions.title: Successful registration

steps.action: Open /register and submit a valid new-account form for round3-authcore-20260401@example.test
steps.expected: Account is created, verification email is sent, and the browser redirects into onboarding
steps.observed: Account was created and a verification email was emitted
The UI stayed on /register and showed the success card 'Account created! Please check your email to verify your account before signing in.'

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log: POST /api/auth/register -> 201; verification email logged for round3-authcore-20260401@example.test

assertions.issues: Registration still does not redirect to onboarding; it remains on /register with a success card.

steps.action: Resubmit the registration form with the existing email round3-authcore-20260401@example.test
steps.expected: A generic non-enumerating error is shown and the request is rejected
steps.observed: The UI showed the generic error 'We could not complete your registration. Please check your details or sign in if you already have an account.'

steps.expected: POST /api/auth/register returns 409 or 422 without exposing account existence

evidence.responseBody: We could not complete your registration. Please check your details or sign in if you already have an account.

steps.action: Enter the weak password 123456 on the registration form
steps.expected: Client-side validation surfaces the password requirements
steps.observed: The page showed the live strength indicator with unmet requirements for at least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, and one special character, plus the inline error 'Password must be at least 8 characters'

assertions.title: Successful login with email and password

steps.action: Log in as the namespace owner after attaching the owner to the namespace org
steps.expected: The app authenticates, issues a session token, and redirects to the default dashboard
steps.observed: POST /api/auth/login succeeded and the browser landed on /round3-authcore-20260401-care/dashboard

assertions.title: Account lockout after repeated failed logins

steps.action: Submit five consecutive incorrect passwords for round3-authcore-lockout-20260401@example.test
steps.expected: The fifth attempt triggers a temporary lockout and a 429 response
steps.observed: The fifth attempt showed 'Account temporarily locked due to too many failed login attempts. Try again in 15 minutes or reset your password.'

steps.expected: 401 for the first four attempts and 429 on the fifth
steps.observed: Shared dev-server log recorded four 401 responses followed by a 429 on the fifth request

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log: POST /api/auth/login -> 401 x4, then 429 on the fifth attempt
evidence.responseBody: Account temporarily locked due to too many failed login attempts. Try again in 15 minutes or reset your password.

steps.action: Click Sign out from the owner dashboard
steps.expected: Session is invalidated and the user is returned to login
steps.observed: The browser returned to /login and the session cookie disappeared

steps.action: Navigate back to the protected dashboard URL after logout
steps.expected: Protected route redirects back to login

steps.action: Request a real password reset email for the owner account and open the logged reset link
steps.observed: The reset form loaded from the shared dev-server email link

steps.action: Submit the form with the new password Round3Core!2
steps.expected: Password updates successfully and the flow returns the user to sign in
steps.observed: The page showed 'Password reset' and 'Redirecting to sign in in 1…', then the login page was used for the follow-up checks

steps.action: Try the old password and then the new password
steps.expected: Old password fails; new password succeeds
steps.observed: The old password was rejected and the new password returned to /round3-authcore-20260401-care/dashboard

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log: POST /api/auth/forgot-password -> 200; POST /api/auth/reset-password -> 200; POST /api/auth/login -> 401 with old password, then 200 with new password

steps.action: Authenticate as the owner and simulate the idle-expiry condition by clearing only the auth session cookie while preserving the long-lived session_hint cookie
steps.expected: The next protected-page request redirects to login with the session-expired message
steps.observed: Opening the protected dashboard redirected to /login?callbackUrl=%2Fround3-authcore-20260401-care%2Fdashboard&reason=session_expired and the page showed 'Your session has expired due to inactivity. Please sign in to continue.'

assertions.title: Permission enforcement — restricted action

steps.action: Log in as the namespace viewer and confirm the viewer can read the org-scoped persons API
steps.expected: Viewer session is valid for read access only
steps.observed: GET /api/persons returned 200 and listed only the namespace person fixture

steps.action: Attempt a direct PUT to /api/persons/92ace0d3-b96f-49be-a577-bc97bb390b9f with the viewer session
steps.expected: API rejects the edit with 403 insufficient permissions
steps.observed: The API returned 405 Method Not Allowed because no PUT handler exists

evidence.network: curl login established an org-scoped viewer session; GET /api/persons -> 200; PUT /api/persons/:id -> 405

assertions.issues: The restricted action path still fails at the routing surface with 405 instead of returning a 403 permission denial for the viewer role.

assertions.title: JWT token cannot be tampered with

steps.action: Capture a real viewer authjs.session-token from a successful login
steps.expected: A valid session token is available for a protected API request
steps.observed: Viewer login returned 200 and the cookie jar contained authjs.session-token

steps.action: Alter the token and send it to GET /api/persons
steps.expected: Protected API rejects the tampered token with 401 Unauthorized
steps.observed: GET /api/persons with the tampered cookie returned 401 Unauthorized and cleared the invalid session token

frictions.description: Opening real verification links with agent-browser produced 'Execution context was destroyed' during the redirect chain.
frictions.resolution: Re-read the final browser URL and confirmed the verification redirect in the shared dev-server log before proceeding.

frictions.description: The assigned assertions needed an org-scoped dashboard and a concrete person ID, but those setup surfaces were not central to this auth retest.
frictions.resolution: After registering and verifying only namespace-scoped accounts, I used a one-off Bun/Drizzle script to create just the namespace org membership and person fixture, then tested through the real login/UI/API surfaces.

frictions.description: The real inactivity timeout is 30 minutes, which is impractical to wait out in-session.
frictions.resolution: Simulated the timeout condition by clearing the auth session cookie while preserving session_hint, then requested a protected route to verify the real session-expired redirect and message.

summary: Tested 10 assertions: 8 passed, 2 failed. Failures: VAL-AUTH-001 (registration still stays on /register instead of redirecting to onboarding) and VAL-AUTH-018 (viewer direct edit attempt returns 405 instead of 403).
